---
title: "Direct Hire Authority in Federal Job Postings"
author: "Abigail Haddad"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: right
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    fig-width: 12
    fig-height: 6
    self-contained: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

Analysis of Direct Hire Authority usage across ~3 million federal job postings
(January 2017 – March 2026), sourced from the
[loyoladatamining/usajobs](https://huggingface.co/datasets/loyoladatamining/usajobs)
HuggingFace dataset. Run `scripts/find_direct_hire.py` first to generate the
input data.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import re
from collections import Counter
from great_tables import GT, md

RESULTS_PATH = "../results/direct_hire_matches.csv"

df = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_PATH)
df['year'] = df['split'].str[:4].astype(int)
df['month'] = df['split'].str[5:].astype(int)
df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['split'].str.replace('_', '-') + '-01')
df['matched_phrase_lower'] = df['matched_phrase'].str.lower().str.strip()

print(f"Total direct hire postings: {len(df):,}")
print(f"Date range: {df['split'].min()} → {df['split'].max()}")
print(f"Unique job titles: {df['title'].nunique():,}")
```

## Overall Stats

```{python}
# We need total postings per split to compute rates — read from a summary file if present,
# otherwise just show raw counts.
summary = pd.DataFrame({
    'Metric': [
        'Direct hire postings found',
        'Date range',
        'Unique job titles',
        'Unique control numbers',
    ],
    'Value': [
        f"{len(df):,}",
        f"{df['split'].min()} → {df['split'].max()}",
        f"{df['title'].nunique():,}",
        f"{df['usajobsControlNumber'].nunique():,}",
    ]
})

GT(summary).tab_header(
    title="Direct Hire Authority: Summary",
    subtitle="loyoladatamining/usajobs HuggingFace dataset"
).tab_options(quarto_disable_processing=True).show()
```

## Phrase Variations

What exact language appears in the postings?

```{python}
phrase_counts = df['matched_phrase_lower'].value_counts().reset_index()
phrase_counts.columns = ['Phrase', 'Count']
phrase_counts['Pct'] = (phrase_counts['Count'] / len(df) * 100).round(1).astype(str) + '%'

GT(phrase_counts.head(30)).tab_header(
    title="Direct Hire Phrase Variations",
    subtitle="Top 30 matched phrases (case-normalized)"
).cols_align('left', columns=['Phrase']).tab_options(quarto_disable_processing=True).show()
```

## Trend Over Time

```{python}
monthly = df.groupby('date').size().reset_index(name='count')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 5))
ax.bar(monthly['date'], monthly['count'], width=20, color='steelblue', alpha=0.8)
ax.set_title('Direct Hire Postings by Month', fontsize=14)
ax.set_xlabel('Month')
ax.set_ylabel('Postings')
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{int(x):,}'))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
yearly = df.groupby('year').size().reset_index(name='Direct Hire Postings')
yearly.columns = ['Year', 'Direct Hire Postings']

GT(yearly).tab_header(
    title="Direct Hire Postings by Year"
).tab_options(quarto_disable_processing=True).show()
```

## Top Job Titles

```{python}
title_counts = df['title'].value_counts().reset_index()
title_counts.columns = ['Job Title', 'Count']

GT(title_counts.head(30)).tab_header(
    title="Top 30 Job Titles with Direct Hire Mentions"
).cols_align('left', columns=['Job Title']).tab_options(quarto_disable_processing=True).show()
```

## Example Contexts

A sample of the matched text in context.

```{python}
sample = df[['title', 'split', 'matched_phrase', 'context']].sample(min(20, len(df)), random_state=42)
sample.columns = ['Title', 'Month', 'Matched Phrase', 'Context']

GT(sample.reset_index(drop=True)).tab_header(
    title="Sample Direct Hire Mentions in Context"
).cols_align('left', columns=['Title', 'Context']).tab_options(quarto_disable_processing=True).show()
```
